figures.caption: Fig. 3. Comparison of the MINLP offline benchmark (black), the trained RL agent (red), the online hybrid RL-MPC method for a horizon of N = 15 laps (blue), and the online hybrid RL-MPC method for a horizon of N ≥ 40 laps (cyan, dashed), with constraint bounds shown as gray dashed lines. The plot shows the fuel allocation as a percentage of the nominal allocation (top), the battery energy allocation where positive values denote charging and negative values discharging (middle), and the pit stop timing with compound selection (bottom).

Fig. 2. Cost-to-go evaluated along the closed-loop trajectory for γ = 0.9999. In the top plot, the critics with ReLU (blue) and Smooth ReLU (red) activation functions approximate C∗(x, u) (black), all decreasing as fewer laps remain. The bottom plot shows that both critics underestimate C∗(x, u) by up to 30 s which is around 0.5% of total race time, an error that becomes increasingly relevant for shorter prediction horizons, where the MPC relies more heavily on the critic to capture longterm performance. The difference between ReLU and SmoothReLU remains negligible along the optimal solution trajectory.

Fig. 1. Hybrid RL-MPC framework. The RL actor is rolled out over the prediction horizon N to supply discrete action trajectories and a continuous warm-start to the MPC, which optimizes continuous inputs with Cθ(x, u c, ud) as terminal cost. Only the first continuous input uc,∗ 0 from the MPC and the first discrete input ud,RL

Fig. 5. Comparison of the hybrid RL-MPC without traffic model (black) and with traffic model (15) (blue), with constraint bounds shown as gray dashed lines. The plot shows the fuel energy allocation (top), battery energy allocation (second), pit stop actions (third), and gap time to the competitor (bottom), where positive values indicate being behind. Both methods retain the nominal pit stop strategy, but only the hybrid RL-MPC with the traffic model modulates its energy allocation immediately after the first pit stop to clear the traffic earlier, finishing 5.19 s ahead of the nominal strategy.
